title: Emergency aid for displaced families
story:
My name is Layla Hassan and I am writing on behalf of my family and twelve neighbouring families in the Kafr Halab district of Aleppo Governorate, Syria. In February 2025, a series of aftershock tremors following the broader regional seismic activity caused severe structural damage to three residential blocks on our street. Four homes collapsed partially, leaving 47 people — including 19 children under the age of 10 — without habitable shelter. My own house lost its eastern load-bearing wall; we have been living in the ground-floor hallway of a partially standing adjacent building owned by our neighbour, Abu Walid Qasem, who has graciously allowed us to stay.

I work as a part-time seamstress and my husband, Tariq Hassan, was a secondary-school mathematics teacher before the school on Al-Nour Street was closed in late 2023 due to safety assessments. We have three children: Nour (9), Rahim (6), and infant Sana (7 months). Our combined monthly income is approximately SYP 320,000 — insufficient to cover temporary rental, food, and medical costs simultaneously.

The immediate needs are: emergency shelter repair materials (cement, rebar, corrugated sheeting) estimated at USD 4,200 for the four most-damaged households; clean water tank replacement for the block (the shared tank cracked in the tremors) at USD 650; and a two-month food-security buffer for all 47 displaced individuals at approximately USD 1,800.

We have contacted the local Aleppo Civil Council office on 14 March 2025 and received a structural damage assessment letter (reference ACC-2025-0847) confirming Category 3 partial collapse for two units and Category 2 damage for two further units. We do not have international NGO support at this time — the nearest active distribution point is in Bab al-Hadid, 11 km away, and transport costs are prohibitive.

All funds collected will be disbursed through my registered local liaison, the Kafr Halab Neighbourhood Relief Committee (registration KH-RC-2019-114), which will issue itemised receipts for every expenditure. I commit to posting a full photo and receipt update within 60 days of each disbursement.
vouching_narrative: The Kafr Halab Neighbourhood Relief Committee (KH-RC-2019-114) can confirm the damage described. Committee chairperson Mr. Bilal Nasser Al-Kurdi (reachable via the Aleppo Civil Council directory) conducted the site walk on 15 March 2025 and countersigned the ACC-2025-0847 assessment letter. Additionally, Dr. Hana Idris, a volunteer physician with the Al-Salam Clinic on Al-Nour Street, treated two of the affected children for respiratory issues caused by dust exposure and can attest to the family's circumstances. No international NGO has formally registered this case in their tracking systems as of the campaign creation date.
